key_quotes: SMU is a small, conservative, greek oriented university that I have come to realize is not for me at all. It took me coming here to realize that I hate the focus on wealth and greek life
Coming in as a junior is quite an unpleasant experience, even for an extravert, on the basis of finding your group outside of greek life
We are Baylor times TCU to the third power. I have more money than you could dream of. My dad has more net worth than Baylor.
key_points: Greek life and wealth dominate social scene significantly
Finding community outside Greek life, especially as transfer, is hard
Multiple students transferred specifically due to wealth-focused culture

key_quotes: I decided to go to community college for financial and mental health reasons

Political science described as genuinely rigorous with demanding professors

key_quotes: They actually mentioned specific extracurriculars. It made me feel like they actually read my application

key_points: Personalized acceptance letters referencing specific extracurriculars impressed applicants
Meadows used AI art in ads instead of hiring students
Administration signals suggest disconnect from school's own stated values

Where there are 232 sunny days and strong medical school connections awaiting you

key_quotes: If in Texas or nearby, SMU. If elsewhere in the SE, NE, or Cali, GT, no question with its reputation

Brand recognition drops significantly outside of Texas
Georgia Tech recommended over SMU for careers beyond Texas

key_quotes: Yes, paying full cost SMU vs A&M, choose A&M. If the price was equal, SMU is the better choice for ROI

Without scholarships, sticker price is very hard to justify

key_quotes: I got direct entry into the Cox School of Business along with a scholarship and entry into the honors program
